text: A smoothie recipe for 4 servings requires 360 ml of orange juice, 200 g of yoghurt, and 3 bananas. Priya wants to make 10 servings using the same recipe. She has 850 ml of orange juice, 480 g of yoghurt, and 8 bananas. Which ingredient will run out first, and how many full servings can she actually make?
options: Orange juice — she can make 9 full servings
Yoghurt — she can make 9 full servings
Bananas — she can make 10 full servings
Orange juice — she can make 8 full servings
Yoghurt — she can make 8 full servings
generatorSolution:
Step 1: Find the amount of each ingredient needed per serving.
• Orange juice: 360 ml ÷ 4 = 90 ml per serving
• Yoghurt: 200 g ÷ 4 = 50 g per serving
• Bananas: 3 ÷ 4 = 0.75 bananas per serving

Step 2: Find how many servings each ingredient can provide.
• Orange juice: 850 ÷ 90 = 9.44... → 9 full servings
• Yoghurt: 480 ÷ 50 = 9.6 → 9 full servings
• Bananas: 8 ÷ 0.75 = 10.66... → 10 full servings

Step 3: The ingredient that allows the fewest servings runs out first. Both orange juice and yoghurt allow 9 full servings, but orange juice runs out slightly sooner (9.44 servings vs 9.6 servings), meaning it is the first ingredient to be completely used up.

Therefore, orange juice runs out first, and Priya can make 9 full servings.
